items.title: [ROADMAP] Help us expand job data — all suggestions welcome!
items.description:
## TL;DR

We want to dramatically expand the job data this project surfaces, and we need **your ideas**. Whether you have a small tweak, a wild idea, or a full proposal — drop it in the comments. **All suggestions are welcome and I will reply to every comment.**

---

## Why this roadmap?

New-Grad-Jobs is most useful when it captures the widest possible surface of legitimate new-grad opportunities. Today we scrape and curate from a known set of sources, but there's a long tail of companies, regions, and job types we're missing.

This issue is the central place to brainstorm **how we add more jobs** — sources, regions, scrapers, partnerships, automation, data quality, anything.

## What kind of suggestions are we looking for?

Pick whatever resonates — these are just prompts:

### Sources
- [ ] Specific ATS platforms we don't yet scrape (Workday tenants, Greenhouse boards, Lever, Ashby, SmartRecruiters, iCIMS, Taleo, Eightfold, Phenom, Jobvite, BrassRing, etc.)
- [ ] Specific companies whose new-grad pipelines we should track
- [ ] Regional job boards (EU, India, LATAM, MENA, APAC, Canada, UK, etc.)
- [ ] University career portals worth aggregating
- [ ] Government / public-sector hiring portals
- [ ] Startup-focused aggregators (YC, Wellfound, etc.)
- [ ] Diversity-focused or accessibility-focused boards

### Job categories
- [ ] Internship pipelines distinct from full-time
- [ ] PhD / research / quant-specific listings
- [ ] Hardware, semiconductor, robotics, aerospace
- [ ] Game industry
- [ ] Finance / trading / actuarial new-grad tracks
- [ ] Bio / pharma / healthcare new-grad roles
- [ ] Trades, ops, supply chain, manufacturing new-grad programs

### Data quality
- [ ] How should we detect & dedupe reposted listings?
- [ ] How should we classify \"new grad\" vs \"junior\" vs \"intern\" more accurately?
- [ ] Should we surface salary data when available? From which sources?
- [ ] Should we flag visa sponsorship / remote / hybrid more explicitly?
- [ ] How should we handle stale postings (last-seen vs first-seen)?
- [ ] What metadata is most useful to filter on?

### Infrastructure & automation
- [ ] Scraper frameworks or libraries worth adopting
- [ ] Anti-bot / rate-limit strategies that respect ToS
- [ ] Workflow scheduling improvements (cron, GitHub Actions, queues)
- [ ] LLM-assisted parsing for unstructured careers pages
- [ ] Storage / dedup / canonicalization approaches
- [ ] Notification channels (RSS, Discord, Slack, email digest)

### Community & contribution
- [ ] How can we make it easier for contributors to add a new company in one PR?
- [ ] Should we publish a contribution template specifically for \"add a company\"?
- [ ] Would a structured YAML/JSON registry of companies + ATS endpoints help?
- [ ] Mentorship for first-time contributors who want to add a scraper

## How to suggest

Just comment below. Any format works:

- **A single company:** \"Please add `<Company>` — their careers page is `<URL>`\"
- **A source idea:** \"There's an ATS called `<X>` used by these companies: …\"
- **A feature idea:** \"It would be great if listings showed `<field>`\"
- **A full proposal:** longer-form design ideas, link to a gist or doc
- **A pain point:** \"As a job seeker, I wish this repo did `<X>`\"

You don't need to know how to scrape, write Python, or anything technical. **Ideas count just as much as code.**

## Commitment

- I'll **reply to every comment on this thread.**
- Concrete, actionable suggestions will be moved into their own tracking issues and linked back here.
- Larger themes will become milestones on the roadmap.
- Credit will go to the suggester in the resulting PR / issue / changelog.

## Ground rules

- Be respectful — this is a community space.
- Don't post anything that violates a source's Terms of Service or robots.txt; we want sustainable, ethical aggregation.
- Don't post personal data, recruiter contacts, or anything that could harm a company or candidate.
- Prefer one idea per comment when possible — it makes follow-up easier.

---

**Thanks in advance — every suggestion helps a new grad find their first job.**

items.title: [UX]: status bar — drop noisy SEL slug, make SAVED count clickable
items.description: ### Description Two coupled changes to the dashboard footer status bar (\`docs/terminal/dashboard.jsx:402–420\`): **1. Drop the \`SEL: …\` indicator.** \`\`\`html SEL: samsara-field-sales-engineer-ii,-enterprise-midwest/northeast-remote---us \`\`\` The selected job's row is already highlighted in the center table and its full detail is rendered in the right panel. Repeating the (very long, kebab-case) job ID in the status bar adds visual noise without conveying anything the user can't see at a glance. **2. Make \`SAVED: N\` clickable.** Today the \`SAVED\` count is a passive indicator. The natural UX expectation — given that everything else in this terminal-style UI is interactive — is that clicking it filters the table to *only* the saved jobs. Click again to clear. ### Proposed behavior - Add a \`savedOnly\` boolean state. - When active, \`preCompanyFiltered\` excludes any job whose id isn't in \`saved\`. - The HIRING NOW sidebar's company counts automatically follow (they already derive from \`preCompanyFiltered\`), so the sidebar collapses to just the companies among the user's saved jobs — which is exactly the cross-section the user wants while pruning. - Visual: when active, the SAVED chip flips to amber bg + black text so it reads as \"on\". Tooltip explains the toggle. Keyboard activation on Enter/Space. - Edge case: if \`saved.size === 0\`, the click flashes a toast (\`\"no saved jobs\"\`) and is a no-op — no point activating a filter that would empty the table. ### Out of scope - URL persistence of the saved-only filter. - A separate \"saved\" tab (the toggle is enough).
items.excerpt: ### Description Two coupled changes to the dashboard footer status bar (\`docs/terminal/dashboard.jsx:402–420\`): **1. Drop the \`SEL: …\` indicator.** \`\`\`html <span>SEL: <span>samsara-field-sales-engineer-ii,-ente...

items.title: [Bug]: POSTED column sort is alphabetical, not chronological
items.description:
### Bug Type
Frontend bug (sort comparator)

### Description

Clicking the **POSTED** column header in the dashboard's job table does fire a sort, but the result is wrong: jobs are ordered alphabetically by the relative-time string (\`\"1d\"\`, \`\"2w\"\`, \`\"3mo\"\`) rather than by actual recency.

Root cause in \`docs/terminal/dashboard.jsx\`:

\`\`\`jsx
if (sortKey === 'posted')   out.sort((a,b) => dir*a.posted.localeCompare(b.posted));
\`\`\`

\`j.posted\` is the formatted output of \`ageString()\` in \`docs/terminal/data.jsx\` — values look like \`\"now\"\`, \`\"3h\"\`, \`\"2d\"\`, \`\"1w\"\`, \`\"2mo\"\`. \`localeCompare\` on those produces:

\`\`\`
\"1d\" < \"1w\" < \"2d\" < \"2w\" < \"30d\" < \"3h\" < \"now\"
\`\`\`

…which has nothing to do with which jobs are actually newer.

### Steps to reproduce
1. Open https://jobs.riteshrana.engineer/
2. Click the **POSTED** column header.
3. Observe rows ordered by alphabet on their relative-time string, not by recency.

### Proposed fix

The raw ISO timestamp (\`j.posted_at\`) is already on the source records — \`ageString(j.posted_at)\` is computed in \`data.jsx\`. Plumb the raw timestamp through onto the mapped \`NGJOBS\` entries (e.g. \`postedTs: new Date(j.posted_at).getTime()\` with \`|| 0\` fallback for missing values), then sort numerically:

\`\`\`jsx
if (sortKey === 'posted') out.sort((a,b) => dir*(b.postedTs - a.postedTs));
\`\`\`

\`dir=1\` (the default on the first click) puts the **newest jobs first**, matching how a new-grad job board reader naturally reads the column (\"recently posted on top\").

### Affected file
\`docs/terminal/dashboard.jsx\` (line ~76), \`docs/terminal/data.jsx\` (line ~134).
items.excerpt: ### Bug Type Frontend bug (sort comparator) ### Description Clicking the **POSTED** column header in the dashboard's job table does fire a sort, but the result is wrong: jobs are ordered alphabetically by the relative...

items.title: [Feature]: clicking a company in HIRING NOW should filter the job list to that company
items.description:
### Bug Type / UX
Frontend interaction

### Description

The \`HIRING NOW\` sidebar on the dashboard (\`docs/terminal/dashboard.jsx\`) lists companies and their open-role counts:

\`\`\`
SpaceX                186
Anduril Industries    164
Waymo                 51
…
\`\`\`

But the rows have no click handler. Users naturally expect that clicking a company name will narrow the center job table to that company and pop the first matching role into the right-hand detail panel — that's the affordance the layout implies.

Today, clicking does nothing. The chrome hints at interactivity (it's a Bloomberg-terminal aesthetic, and every other row in the UI is clickable) but the wire-up is missing.

### Steps to reproduce
1. Open https://jobs.riteshrana.engineer/
2. Scroll to the \`HIRING NOW\` panel in the left sidebar.
3. Click any company (e.g. \`SpaceX\`).
4. Nothing happens.

### Proposed solution

- Add \`company\` to the dashboard \`filters\` state (a \`Set\` so multi-select works).
- Filter \`filtered\` jobs by \`filters.company\` if non-empty.
- Compute \`coCounts\` from the **pre-company** filter set so the sidebar list does **not** collapse to just the selected company — users can still switch between companies.
- Wire each HIRING NOW row to toggle its company in the set; clicking the same company again deselects.
- Visual selected state on the row (background + amber accent on text) and \`cursor: pointer\` to make the affordance discoverable.
- Auto-select the first matching job for the detail panel (the existing \`useEffect\` already handles this when \`filtered\` changes).

### Out of scope
- Multi-select keyboard nav.
- Reflecting the company filter in the URL.
- A separate "company picker" modal — the sidebar list already serves this role.

items.title: [Bug]: /favicon.ico returns 404 (no favicon configured)
items.description:
### Bug Type
Other (frontend)

### Description

Browsers auto-request \`/favicon.ico\` and the deployed site at https://jobs.riteshrana.engineer/ responds with **404**:

\`\`\`
GET https://jobs.riteshrana.engineer/favicon.ico → 404
\`\`\`

\`docs/\` does not contain a \`favicon.ico\` (or \`favicon.svg\`), and \`docs/index.html\` has no \`<link rel=\"icon\">\` tag. Cosmetic only — the browser tab shows a blank icon and the console shows a 404 entry on every page load.

### Steps to Reproduce

1. Open https://jobs.riteshrana.engineer/ in any browser.
2. Open DevTools → Network or Console.
3. Observe the \`/favicon.ico\` request returns 404.
4. Note the tab icon is blank.

### Additional Context

Discovered while auditing the live site after #299–#304. Predates this work; not a regression.

Suggested fix:
- Add a small brand-aligned SVG favicon to \`docs/favicon.svg\` (amber accent + dark terminal aesthetic, consistent with the LIVE-chip dot and the rest of the UI).
- Add \`<link rel=\"icon\" type=\"image/svg+xml\" href=\"favicon.svg\">\` to \`docs/index.html\`.
- Optionally include a \`favicon.ico\` PNG fallback for legacy browsers, but modern browsers all support SVG favicons.

items.title: Flaky test: test_unicorn_company can fail when iteration order picks a FAANG+ unicorn
items.description:
## Summary

`tests/test_enrichment.py::TestGetCompanyTier::test_unicorn_company` is order-dependent and fails non-deterministically depending on Python's hash randomization.

## Where

`tests/test_enrichment.py` (around lines 140–146):

```python
def test_unicorn_company(self):
    from update_jobs import UNICORNS
    if UNICORNS:
        company = next(iter(UNICORNS))
        result = get_company_tier(company)
        assert result['tier'] == 'unicorn'
```

## Why it flakes

`get_company_tier()` in `scripts/update_jobs.py` checks `FAANG_PLUS` **before** `UNICORNS`:

```python
if company_name in FAANG_PLUS:
    tier_info = {'tier': 'faang_plus', ...}
elif company_name in UNICORNS:
    tier_info = {'tier': 'unicorn', ...}
```

When a company is in **both** sets and ends up first in `iter(UNICORNS)` for a given `PYTHONHASHSEED`, the test asserts `'unicorn'` but receives `'faang_plus'`.

This was observed in CI on PR #295:

```
AssertionError: assert 'faang_plus' == 'unicorn'
  - unicorn
  + faang_plus
```

A re-run with a different hash seed passed, confirming flakiness rather than logic regression. PR #295 only changed `normalize_date_string()` and did not touch UNICORNS/FAANG_PLUS at all.

## Proposed fix

Pick deterministically from companies that are in `UNICORNS` but **not** in `FAANG_PLUS`:

```python
def test_unicorn_company(self):
    from update_jobs import UNICORNS, FAANG_PLUS
    unicorn_only = sorted(UNICORNS - FAANG_PLUS)
    assert unicorn_only, "Expected at least one unicorn that is not also FAANG+"
    result = get_company_tier(unicorn_only[0])
    assert result['tier'] == 'unicorn'
```

`sorted(...)` removes set-iteration-order nondeterminism; `UNICORNS - FAANG_PLUS` removes the tier-precedence ambiguity.

Same hardening should be applied to `test_finance_sector_detected` and `test_defense_sector_detected` — those assertions are sector-based (additive, not precedence-gated), so they happen to be safe today, but sorting makes them robust to future refactors.

## Impact

- Spurious CI failures on unrelated PRs (cost: re-runs and reviewer trust).
- Erodes confidence in the test suite.

items.title: [Bug]: Operator Manual is out of sync with current config and workflow behavior
items.description:
### Bug Type
Other

### Description
`docs/wiki/Operator-Manual.md` contains stale instructions that no longer match repository behavior.

Examples:
- It references `filters.exclude_keywords`, but current config uses `filtering.exclusion_signals`.
- It suggests tuning thread pools directly in `scripts/update_jobs.py`, while worker-pool behavior is now configurable via `config.yml` (`worker_pools`).
- It references JobSpy location/config patterns that no longer match current configuration shape.
- Several workflow guidance notes are stale relative to `.github/workflows/update-jobs.yml`.

This creates contributor confusion and increases maintenance error risk.

### Steps to Reproduce
1. Open `docs/wiki/Operator-Manual.md`.
2. Compare recipe/config field names against `config.yml`.
3. Compare workflow instructions against `.github/workflows/update-jobs.yml`.
4. Observe mismatches between documented and actual behavior.

### Additional Context
Candidate source-of-truth files for alignment:
- `docs/wiki/Operator-Manual.md`
- `config.yml`
- `.github/workflows/update-jobs.yml`
- `scripts/update_jobs.py`

items.title: [Bug]: market-history category breakdown is always empty despite non-empty jobs
items.description:
### Bug Type
Other

### Description
`docs/market-history.json` snapshots are being generated with an empty `categories` object, even when `docs/jobs.json` has populated category counts.

This makes trend analytics incomplete and causes the Insights pipeline to lose category-level historical signal.

### Steps to Reproduce
1. Run the scraper (`python scripts/update_jobs.py`).
2. Open `docs/jobs.json` and verify `meta.categories` contains category counts.
3. Open `docs/market-history.json`.
4. Observe latest snapshot has `"categories": {}`.

### Additional Context
Likely root cause in `save_market_history()` (`scripts/update_jobs.py`):
- code iterates `job.get('categories', [])`
- enriched jobs store a singular `job['category']` object, not `categories`

Suggested fix direction:
- aggregate from `job.get('category', {}).get('id')`
- add regression tests for snapshot category population

items.title: 🚨 Board is stale — scraper may have stopped
items.description:
## 🚨 Automated Alert: Board is Stale

The watchdog detected that `docs/jobs.json` has not been updated in **93 minutes**.

| Field | Value |
|-------|-------|
| Last updated | `2026-03-18T01:05:29Z` |
| Stale for | 93 minutes |
| Update cycle | Every 5 minutes |

## What to check

1. **Actions tab**: Is the `Update New Grad Jobs` workflow failing? Check [Actions](https://github.com/ambicuity/New-Grad-Jobs/actions/workflows/update-jobs.yml)
2. **Branch protection**: Did a new ruleset get added that blocks direct push?
3. **Scraper errors**: Is the scraper producing 0 jobs (all sources failing)?

## Auto-close

Close this issue once the board is confirmed fresh again.

*Filed automatically by the Board Freshness Watchdog workflow.*

items.title: [Test] E2E Agent Triage Verification
items.description: The Workday API is returning HTTP 422 errors for about 30 enterprise companies. The scraper silently drops these jobs. Can someone investigate?
items.excerpt: The Workday API is returning HTTP 422 errors for about 30 enterprise companies. The scraper silently drops these jobs. Can someone investigate?

items.title: [Beginner]: Add `timeout` as a parameter to fetch functions
items.description:
### Description
Currently, HTTP requests in `update_jobs.py` have hardcoded `timeout=5`. Change fetch functions to accept a `timeout` parameter, defaulting to a `DEFAULT_TIMEOUT = 5` constant.

### Why it matters
Hardcoded timeouts make it difficult to adapt to temporary API slowdowns. Parameterizing it allows dynamic adjustment.

### Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Create `DEFAULT_TIMEOUT` constant
- [ ] Update function signatures and `HTTP_SESSION.get()` calls to use it
- [ ] Passes `pre-commit run --all-files`

items.title: [Beginner]: Add argparse to generate_companies.py for command-line options
items.description: Currently, generate_companies.py hardcodes the number of companies to generate (e.g., 4000, 1900, 1300) in the __main__ block. This issue is to use argparse to allow these to be passed as command-line arguments (e.g., --greenhouse-count 4000).

items.title: [Beginner]: Add type hints to generate_companies.py
items.description: The functions generate_greenhouse_companies, generate_lever_companies, generate_workday_companies, and format_yaml_companies currently lack Python type hints. This issue involves adding valid type hints (e.g., count: int, start_id: int, returning list[dict[str, str]]) to improve code readability and static analysis.
